facts.value: Alibaba and Tencent (strategic equity investors / Chinese cloud providers)
facts.excerpt: Alibaba Group Holding and Tencent Holdings are among a band of prominent Chinese Big Tech companies and venture capital (VC) firms that have helped start-up Beijing Zhipu Huazhang Technology Co (Zhipu
facts.notes: This is an EQUITY INVESTMENT relationship (Alibaba, Tencent, Ant Group, Meituan, Xiaomi, HongShan, GL Ventures et al.), NOT a disclosed compute-supply or cloud-lease contract. The article names no cloud/compute infrastructure terms. Included because Alibaba Cloud/Tencent Cloud are the lab's known partners; no rented MW capacity is attributable to Zhipu from this. Amount US$342M is total 2023 fundraising, not infrastructure capex.

verification.verifier_note: SCMP (Oct 20, 2023) states 'Alibaba Group Holding and Tencent Holdings are among a band of prominent Chinese Big Tech companies... [that] helped start-up... Zhipu AI raise a total of 2.5 billion yuan (US$342 million)' and both took part in the Series B3 round. Matches value, site, and date.

facts.value: Huawei (Z.ai/GLM models adapted to run on Huawei Ascend and Kirin chips; cloud-device collaboration)
facts.excerpt: The tie-up marks a major breakthrough in cloud-device collaboration between home-grown large [language] models and computational architecture, highlighting the deeper integration of a domestic AI ecos
facts.notes: Hardware/ecosystem partnership with Huawei (Ascend server chips + Kirin device chips), not Zhipu-owned capacity. No MW, chip count, or datacenter site given. English secondary reporting; translation/secondary-source uncertainty.

verification.verifier_note: SCMP (Aug 9, 2025) states GLM models are 'now compatible with Huawei's Ascend processors... and Kirin chips' and describes 'a major breakthrough in cloud-device collaboration.' Matches value, site, and date.

facts.value: Huawei Ascend Atlas 800T A2 (used to train Zhipu's GLM-Image model)
facts.excerpt: Huawei's Ascend Atlas 800T A2 server, incorporating the company's in-house Ascend AI processors ... MindSpore, Huawei's all-in-one machine learning framework
facts.notes: Compute HARDWARE attributed to Huawei, NOT a Zhipu-owned facility. Zhipu (US Entity List since Jan 2025) trains on Huawei Ascend instead of Nvidia. No chip count, cluster location, power (MW), or datacenter site disclosed. English secondary reporting on a Chinese-language disclosure; translation/secondary-source uncertainty.

verification.verifier_note: SCMP (Jan 14, 2026) literally states 'the entire training pipeline for GLM-Image, from data preparation to the final training run, was conducted on Huawei's Ascend Atlas 800T A2 server' using Huawei Ascend processors and MindSpore. Value, site (huawei-ascend), and date all match.

facts.value: Huawei (GLM-5 deep inference adaptation on Chinese-made chip clusters)
facts.excerpt: Zhipu AI's new GLM-5 large language model has completed deep inference adaptation with Huawei, ensuring the model's stable operation on Chinese-made chip clusters.
facts.notes: Describes inference deployment on domestic chip clusters (also adapted to Moore Threads, Cambricon), NOT a Zhipu-owned cluster. Widely reported in secondary/tech-blog sources that GLM-5 was TRAINED on ~100,000 Huawei Ascend 910B chips, but that figure is not confirmed by Zhipu and I could not verify it verbatim on a primary/reputable page, so it is NOT recorded as a gpu_count. Chinese-lab disclosure; secondary-source uncertainty.

verification.verifier_note: Caixin (Feb 12, 2026) states 'GLM-5 large language model has completed deep inference adaptation with Huawei, ensuring the model's stable operation on Chinese-made chip clusters.' Matches value, site, and date exactly.

facts.excerpt: in early talks with domestic chip-design companies about tailored AI processors
facts.notes: EXPLORATORY ONLY, not a committed program. Per The Information (via SCMP and TrendForce 2026-07-08), Zhipu is in preliminary/early talks with domestic chip-design houses about a custom ASIC for the GLM family; no partner selected (Cambricon and Biren cited only as candidate designers), and a viable ASIC is estimated at >2 years out. Motivated by GLM-5.2 usage surging ~27x and Nvidia export restrictions. Secondary sourcing (SCMP/TrendForce citing The Information); no in-house tapeout, foundry, or chip announced.

verification.verifier_note: SCMP (Jul 9, 2026) reports Zhipu was only 'in early talks with domestic chip-design companies about tailored AI processors' per The Information. This is exploratory/preliminary talks, NOT an 'announced' custom-silicon program. Status mismatch: fact claims status='announced' but the page supports at
